arts.ttl: Frank Lampard sets out Coventry City's FA Cup stance 12 months on from Doug King demand
arts.sum: This time last year Mark Robins was told by Doug King that he wanted an FA Cup run and the former Coventry City manager duly delivered, guiding the Sky Blues to within millimetres of making the final. A self confessed traditionalist whose history and memory of the Cup goes back to watching his dad, Frank Lampard Snr, help lift the trophy for West Ham United when just a toddler in the 1980 final win over Arsenal, the head coach knows his own mind on such matters.

arts.ttl: Frank Lampard on Coventry City’s ‘very strategic’ approach to January transfers
arts.sum: Get the latest Coventry City news sent straight to your phone by joining our Sky Blues WhatsApp group. While the Sky Blues boss insists the club are taking a “very strategic” approach to their mid-term recruitment and not ruling out deals for the longer term, he admits he’d like fresh players in to help at this moment in time.

arts.ttl: Coventry City injury blow as key man ruled out for 'weeks' as others remain sidelined
arts.sum: Providing an update on his squad ahead of Saturday’s FA Cup third round match with Sheffield Wednesday at the CBS Arena, head coach Frank Lampard revealed: “Ben’s unfortunately had a muscle injury against Norwich, so he’s going to be out for some weeks, probably between four to six weeks, possibly, which is unfortunate because obviously what Ben is as a player. It can happen to any player at any time over busy periods, unfortunately, so we will support Ben as much as we can.

arts.ttl: Wing-back 'ready to Czech out' as Frank Lampard contemplates first transfer as Coventry City boss
arts.sum: But the Africa Foot website via Sport Witness claims Ghali, who can also operate as a winger, is being courted by clubs in both England and Italy - with the Sky Blues amongst them. Sport Witness adds Ghali has been with Slovan Liberec since 2022 and has a deal that runs to 2027 after signing an extension last summer, but that both club and player are willing to listen to offers.

arts.ttl: Ipswich Town and Coventry City make approaches for January signing – Player could ‘soon pack his bags’
arts.sum: Ipswich Town and Coventry City are in the picture to sign Slovan Liberec’s Ahmad Ghali in the winter market. The 24-year-old arrived at the Czech club in 2022 and has mainly been used as a wing-back this season, where he made 19 appearances.
